description:
Their second album, People to Meet, Places to See, is a sonic diary of life on
the road—each track capturing a city, mood, or moment from their India tours.
From the laid-back charm of Cubbon Park to the pulsating rhythm of Techno
Police, the record weaves cinematic grooves that invite movement and joy.
Following acclaimed singles Jonty Patel and Ratna Mahal, the People to Meet,
Places to See – Album Launch marks a milestone in their journey, showcasing
their evolution as one of India’s most dynamic and genre-bending live acts.

address.streetAddress: Windmills Craftworks, Raheja Commerzone, Knowledge City, Rai Durg, Hyderabad - 500032Hyderabad

name: Windmills Music Night - Derek & The Cats -HYD

performer.description: Derek & The Cats is a Bangalore-based band led by Derek Mathias, known for their infectious blend of jazz, funk, and house. Renowned for their high-energy instrumental performances, the ensemble brings a fresh, dance-driven sound to every stage.
performer.name: Derek & The Cats

description:
His NAACP Image Award–nominated album Creole Orchestra topped JazzWeek’s 2024
charts, affirming his role as a leading contemporary jazz innovator. Beyond
the stage, he mentors emerging artists as Professor of Studio Music and Jazz
at the University of Miami Frost School of Music, shaping future generations
through scholarship and sound.
name: Windmills Music Night - Etienne Charles - Creole Soul - BLR

performer.description: Trinidad-born trumpeter, composer, and cultural storyteller Etienne Charles creates music that uncovers overlooked histories and celebrates community memory. Praised for his warm tone, layered orchestrations, and rhythmic vitality rooted in Caribbean traditions, his work bridges jazz with social narrative. Through projects such as San Juan Hill – A New York Story, Gullah Roots, and Carnival: The Sound of a People, Charles transforms archival research into immersive performance, amplifying voices often absent from mainstream history.
performer.name: Etienne Charles - Creole Soul

description:
Their critically acclaimed debut album, Afro Anatolian Tales, earned
international recognition, including praise from Charlie Gillett and a five-
star review from Songlines Magazine. Renowned for their immersive and
constantly evolving live performances, the group collaborates with artists,
festivals, and venues across the globe to create unique experiences that
seamlessly blend tradition, innovation, and storytelling through sound. Their
latest production, Tarab Dance, is set to premiere alongside an international
tour in 2026.
name: Windmills Music Night - Afro Anatolian Tales-BLR

performer.description: Afro Anatolian Tales is an Amsterdam-based music collective founded by Şahin During, weaving together ancient Silk Road musical traditions with contemporary electronics, improvisation, and cinematic soundscapes. Drawing inspiration from the rich cultures of Anatolia, Iran, India, and beyond, the ensemble creates a compelling musical dialogue between East and West.
performer.name: Afro Anatolian Tales

description:
At the center of the trio is the extraordinary Delvon Lamarr — a self-taught
multi-instrumentalist blessed with perfect pitch. Originally a trumpeter and
drummer, Lamarr discovered the Hammond B-3 organ at the age of 22 and quickly
developed a signature style marked by warmth, virtuosity, and irresistible
groove. His deep musical instinct and dynamic performances are the heartbeat
of the band.
name: Windmills Music Night - Delvon Lamarr Organ Trio - BLR

performer.description: Delvon Lamarr Organ Trio (DLO3) is on a mission to create music that simply makes people feel good. Rooted in the rich traditions of 1960s organ jazz, the trio draws inspiration from legends like Jimmy Smith and Baby Face Willette, while blending in the soulful grooves of Booker T. & The M.G.’s and The Meters. Their sound effortlessly weaves together elements of Motown, Stax Records soul, blues, and classic funk, delivering an electrifying and deeply soulful live experience.
performer.name: Delvon Lamarr Organ Trio
